title: 第 90 節
en: Tapasa, the Vedic ascetics, and the Shamans, 222. Tapio, Finnish forest-god, 271. Taramaakiaki, sea-weed, 36. Taramahetonga, south-wind, 36. Tarawi, sun, and Tnta, 655, 662. Tartaros and Tnta, 668. Tavun-ozai's, Mordvinian god of swine, Tchim-Paz, Erzjanian name for Chka'i, — see Chkai".
Tchouvan, first Tsar of the Mordvines, Tegni, Mongolian god, also called Chormusda (Ormazd), 254.
Tengri, Mongolian name for the gods, — supreme god of the Mongols, 160 n.
— the same as Tegni ? 426. Teraphim, explained as fetishes, 196. Terhenetiir, mist or fog, Finnish goddess, 268.
— mother of Helios, 652. Themis, the old goddess of justice, — daughter of Helios, 152.
— and deva, deus, 390, 394. Theriolatry andtotemism, 7, 199, 201. Theseus, 180.
— and the birth of the morning, 526.

— fight of the Dioskouroi against, Thetis, wedding feast of Peleus and, Thirty-three gods, 475. Thorr, see Thunar. Thraetaona = Traitana, 2, 672. Thridi, name of Odinn, 671. Thunar milks his heavenly cows (the clouds), 93.
— or Thorr, Indra compared with, — his fight with giants and demons, Thunder, enigmas on the, 100.
— the old man beyond the great water, 10 1. Thyria, a nymph beloved by Apollon, Thyro, a nymph beloved by Apollon, Tiele, Prof., 3?, 34, 36.
— on the physical character of the Vedic gods, 445. Tien, Chinese god of the sky, 261. Tivar, and Devas, 138. Totem, clan-mark, 198.

86l Totemism, supposed to be primitive, — its proper place in mythology, 443. Totems, Dr. Codrington on, 7. Toumo-oza'is, Mordvinian god of the oak-trees, 244. Traitana and Thraetaona, and Feri- dun, 2, 672. Transmigration of souls, 832. in India, Egypt, and Greece, Tratri, an abstract deity, 820, 824. Tree-worship, Finnish, 273. Triptolemos, the inventor of the plough, 536. Trita and Tarawi, 655, 662. Trifca or Tn'ta, the last light of the sun, 661-673.
